display.purpose: Full-screen brand statements, uppercase, one word per line (welcome screen).

headline.purpose: Screen-level headings, often two-tone navy + gold.

body.purpose: Default reading copy, descriptions, form values.

label.purpose: Form labels, chip text, small UI labels.

shadows.name: card-lift
shadows.purpose: The system's only shadow — separates grouped content (product cards) from the page without reading as a floating panel.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Default action button — navy fill, white label, sharp radius.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { height: 56px; padding: 0 24px; background: #0f2743; color: #ffffff; border: none; border-radius: 4px; font-family: 'Inter', sans-serif; font-weight: 600; font-size: 16px; transition: opacity 0.15s; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { opacity: 0.9; } .ds-btn-primary:active { opacity: 0.85; } .ds-btn-primary:disabled { opacity: 0.5; }

components.name: Accent Button
components.description: The single most important CTA on a screen — gold fill, pill radius.
components.css: .ds-btn-accent { height: 56px; padding: 0 24px; background: #eaaa34; color: #0f2743; border: none; border-radius: 29px; font-family: 'Inter', sans-serif; font-weight: 600; font-size: 16px; transition: opacity 0.15s; } .ds-btn-accent:hover { opacity: 0.9; } .ds-btn-accent:active { opacity: 0.85; }

components.name: Outline Button
components.description: Secondary action alongside a primary/accent button.
components.css: .ds-btn-outline { height: 56px; padding: 0 24px; background: #ffffff; color: #0f2743; border: 1.5px solid #ebede9; border-radius: 4px; font-family: 'Inter', sans-serif; font-weight: 600; font-size: 16px; }

components.name: Text Field
components.description: Default text input — off-white fill, hairline border.
components.css: .ds-field { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 8px; } .ds-field-label { font-family: 'Inter', sans-serif; font-weight: 600; font-size: 13px; color: #0f2743; } .ds-field-input { height: 52px; padding: 0 14.5px; background: #f5f6f4; border: 1.5px solid #ebede9; border-radius: 4px; font-family: 'Inter', sans-serif; font-size: 14px; color: #0f2743; } .ds-field-input:focus { outline: none; border-color: #0f2743; } .ds-field-input.ds-error { border-color: #d64545; }

components.name: Chip
components.css: .ds-chip { height: 38px; padding: 0 16px; border-radius: 4px; border: 1.5px solid; font-family: 'Inter', sans-serif; font-weight: 500; font-size: 13px; } .ds-chip-active { background: #0f2743; border-color: #0f2743; color: #ffffff; } .ds-chip-inactive { background: #ffffff; border-color: #ebede9; color: #0f2743; }

components.name: Product Card
components.description: Grouped product summary — the system's only elevated surface.
components.html:
Cotton Twill Fabric
Accra Textiles Ltd.
GHS 45.00
components.css: .ds-card { background: #f1f2ee; border-radius: 4px; padding: 8px; box-shadow: 0 4px 14px rgba(15,38,67,0.07); } .ds-card-thumb { height: 100px; border-radius: 4px; background: #ebede9; } .ds-card-name { margin-top: 8px; font-family: 'Inter', sans-serif; font-weight: 600; font-size: 14px; color: #0f2743; } .ds-card-manufacturer { margin-top: 5px; font-family: 'Inter', sans-serif; font-size: 11px; color: #0f2743; } .ds-card-price { margin-top: 6px; font-family: 'Inter', sans-serif; font-weight: 800; font-size: 15px; color: #eaaa34; }

components.name: Badge
components.description: Compact status/value label — solid brand or tonal semantic fill.
components.css: .ds-badge { padding: 4px 8px; border-radius: 4px; font-family: 'Inter', sans-serif; font-weight: 800; font-size: 10px; letter-spacing: 0.3px; display: inline-block; } .ds-badge-gold { background: #eaaa34; color: #0f2743; } .ds-badge-success { background: #e0f2e3; color: #1a8040; }

narrative.overview: Zyntra's visual system reads like a well-kept ledger book: deep navy as the ink of record, gold as the mark of value, everything laid out with the discipline of something that has to be trusted. This isn't a consumer shopping app dressed up for wholesale — it's infrastructure for people conducting real trade, and the interface should carry that weight without ever feeling heavy-handed. Bold, uppercase extraBold display type ("BEYOND FACTORY GATES", "HOW WILL YOU USE ZYNTRA?") gives the brand its confident, industrial voice; a restrained off-white/card-gray body keeps the working screens (browsing, inventory, orders) legible and calm underneath it. The system explicitly rejects the generic SaaS-dashboard look: no flat gray panels, no interchangeable enterprise-tool chrome. Every surface should feel like it belongs to a single deliberate brand, whether it's a manufacturer's inventory screen or a distributor's tracking view.
keyCharacteristics: Navy-and-gold as the load-bearing palette — navy for structure and trust, gold for value and action
Bold extraBold uppercase display type for identity moments; restrained Inter body copy for working screens
Sharp 4px radius as the default component shape — structural, not soft
Shadows used sparingly and quietly — never a decorative flourish

rules.name: The Two-Color Rule
rules.body: Navy and gold carry the brand identity; every other color in the system is neutral or semantic. If a new UI element needs a color and it isn't structural (navy) or an action/value marker (gold), it belongs in the neutral or semantic tier — never invent a third "brand" hue.

rules.name: The Uppercase Declaration Rule
rules.body: ExtraBold uppercase type is reserved for identity moments — welcome screens, role/section headlines — never for body copy or buttons beyond a short label. Overusing it flattens its impact.

rules.name: The One Shadow Rule
rules.body: There is exactly one elevation value in this system. Don't introduce a second, heavier shadow for "more important" cards — importance is a color/type problem, not a depth problem.

dos: Do keep the palette to navy + gold as the only two brand colors; everything else is neutral or semantic (see The Two-Color Rule).
Do use the 4px sharp radius as the default component shape; save the pill radius for the one accent CTA per screen.
Do use extraBold uppercase type only for identity-level headlines, not body copy or routine buttons (The Uppercase Declaration Rule).
Do keep elevation to the single quiet card-lift shadow; signal importance through color/type, not deeper shadows (The One Shadow Rule).
Do meet WCAG AA contrast (≥4.5:1 body, ≥3:1 large text) and ≥44×44pt touch targets, per PRODUCT.md's accessibility commitment.
donts: Don't build flat, gray, interchangeable enterprise-SaaS-dashboard panels — PRODUCT.md names this directly as the anti-reference; every surface should read as Zyntra's own ledger-and-trade system, not generic admin tooling.
Don't reach for e-commerce/shopping-cart visual tropes (large lifestyle imagery, "add to bag" playfulness) — this is B2B wholesale trade, not consumer retail.
Don't introduce a third brand hue outside navy/gold, or a second shadow depth beyond the single card-lift value.
Don't use light gray body text for anything readable — Zyntra's text ramp bottoms out at Ink Faint (#b2b8bd) for the least important labels only, never for body copy that needs to be read.
